entities.description: bookrag is an agent based approach proposed to address complex document queries by planning and executing operations on a bookindex
bookindex is the data structure or system on which bookrag executes operations for document queries
agent based planning is a core mechanism in bookrag that formulates strategies for operations
structured execution is a core mechanism in bookrag that includes the retrieval process based on ift and generation principles
ift is a principle used within the structured execution mechanism of bookrag
modal type filtering is an operation mentioned as necessary for addressing complex real world document queries
semantic selection is an operation mentioned as necessary for addressing complex real world document queries
multi hop reasoning is an operation mentioned as necessary for addressing complex real world document queries
generation is a process included within the structured execution mechanism of bookrag

relations.description: bookrag executes operations on the bookindex to handle document queries
bookrag utilizes agent based planning as one of its two core mechanisms to formulate strategies
bookrag utilizes structured execution as one of its two core mechanisms to handle retrieval and generation
structured execution includes the retrieval process under the principles of ift
real world document queries necessitate operations like modal type filtering
real world document queries necessitate operations like semantic selection
real world document queries necessitate operations like multi hop reasoning
structured execution includes the generation process as part of its workflow
